name: Q1146593
abstract: Robert Dewees "Cutty" Cutshall (December 29, 1911 – August 16, 1968) was an American jazz trombonist. Cutshall was born in Huntington Co., Pennsylvania, on December 29, 1911.

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians
Midnight Performance: Salute to Eddie Condon

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Muranyi
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Cutty. I played with him you know, more than a few times at the Eddie Condon’s in midtown in the 50s.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Muranyi interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Varro
about_quotes.evidence: playing intermission with Buzzy Drootin and the band consisted of Wild Bill, Peanuts, Cutty Cutshall, George Wettling, Gene Schroeder and Arvell Shaw maybe or Walter Page
about_quotes.doc_title: Johnny Varro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: That was with Wild Bill -- Wild Bill Davison, Edmund Hall, Cutty Cutshall, Schroeder Gene Shrader ... And we played with that band

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: Then we added Bob Wilber and Cutty Cutshall and we went out and we always had that good quartet, you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Roswell Rudd
about_quotes.evidence: our main connection would be the acoustical model, the living model would have been the Eddie Condon guys in New York City. And that would be Wild Bill Davison, Pee Wee Russell, Vic Dickinson, Cutty Cutshall, all those great trombonists, and George Wettling.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roswell Rudd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: George Masso
about_quotes.evidence: Plus there were so many great players in those days, you know there was Jack Teagarden and Cutty Cutshall and Bill Harris and Jack Jenny and Trummy Young. I mean I had all these great players to listen to.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Masso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997
